guidance: Dogs benefit from regular exercise for physical health, mental stimulation, and boredom reduction. Exercise needs vary by breed, age, and health status.
Adult dogs often do well on a consistent feeding schedule with portions adjusted to size and activity. Fresh water should always be available.
Regular brushing helps keep a dog's coat clean and can reduce shedding. Bathing frequency depends on the dog, but overbathing is usually unnecessary.
Vet-related tasks should stay on schedule because follow-up care and preventive visits can affect ongoing health monitoring.
Medication routines for cats should stay consistent and calm. If a dose is missed or difficult to give, the owner should contact a veterinarian for guidance.
source_title: Cornell Feline Health Center Medication & Care

guidance: Cats need clean food and water dishes with fresh contents. Consistent meal routines can help owners notice appetite changes sooner.
source_title: Cornell Feline Health Center Choosing and Caring for Your New Cat

guidance: Cats benefit from regular enrichment and play to support activity and reduce boredom-related behavior issues.
Cats are sensitive to litter box cleanliness, and a clean setup can help maintain normal bathroom habits.
source_title: Cornell Feline Health Center Choosing and Caring for Your New Cat

guidance: Fresh water should be available at all times because hydration is essential for everyday health.
Medication-related tasks should be treated as high importance. Owners should avoid guessing on missed-dose handling and should ask a veterinarian when unsure.
source_title: Cornell Feline Health Center Medication & Care

guidance: Consistent daily routines can make it easier to track changes in appetite, activity, and behavior over time.
When health-related tasks stack up or are skipped, the plan should clearly flag them so the owner can reassess timing and follow up promptly.
